question.text: If option sellers are at a 'fundamental advantage' over option buyers, what explains this?

answer.text: Time value inherently decays to zero by expiry, benefiting sellers who receive premium upfront
Sellers can modify the strike price during the contract
Sellers never lose money on option trades
SEBI provides additional margin protection for sellers

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Of the two premium components, time value inherently declines to zero by expiry (time decay). Since sellers receive premium upfront, this erosion works in their favor — the option's value decreases over time. **The Trap:** Sellers can't modify strikes. Sellers CAN lose money (potentially unlimited for naked sellers). SEBI doesn't provide special protection. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers the seller's time decay advantage. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This structural advantage explains why many professional traders are net option sellers — they earn from time decay while managing risk through hedging and position sizing.
